summary: Re-run the two audit agents whose prompts lost the construction spec to an interpolation bug

findings.claim: Move classification (Task 1): 6 straightedge, 5 collapsing-legal circles, 4 rigid-compass transfers
findings.detail: Straightedge (a): moves 2, 4, 6, 8, 12, 15 — each is a line through two already-constructed points. Collapsing-legal (b): move 1 (about R through O), move 3 (about R through X — 'radius XR' with center R IS through-X, hence collapsing-legal), move 9 (about Z' through O), move 10 (about O through O''), move 11 (about O'' through O). Rigid transfers (c): move 5 (center Z, radius OR), move 7 (center Z', radius XR), move 13 (center Z', radius FF'), move 14 (center Z', radius UH). In each transfer the center is not an endpoint of the radius segment, and I verified none can be reinterpreted as collapsing-legal: no already-constructed point lies at the required distance from the center at that stage (e.g. at move 5 the distances from Z to all 9 available points are {0.125, 0.375, 0.484, 0.875, 0.944, 1.875, ...} — none equals 1; at move 7 none of the 12 available points is at 0.5 from Z'). So the classification in the spec is honest: exactly 4 moves genuinely need the rigid compass.

findings.claim: Transfers legitimized by compass equivalence, Euclid I.2/I.3 (Task 2)
findings.detail: Each transfer 'circle about A with radius BC' is constructible per I.2: build the I.1 equilateral apex D on AB (2 circles), draw rays DA, DB (2 lines), circle about B through C meeting DB produced at G (1 circle), circle about D through G meeting DA produced at L (1 circle), then circle about A through L (1 circle) — 7 primitive moves generically, sometimes 8 with case handling; the segment BC never needs a separate line here because every transferred segment already lies on a drawn line (OR and XR on line PR; FF' on the move-2 line; UH on the move-8 line OU, since U and H both lie on line OU). Exact expansion cost: each transfer 1 → ~7 moves, i.e. ~+6 each, so strict-Euclid total ≈ 15 + 4×6 = ~39 moves (range ~39–47 allowing I.2 case splits). Constructibility is unaffected — the same points are produced.

findings.claim: Dependency order is clean — nothing used before it exists (Task 3, part 1)
findings.detail: Traced every reference: M1 uses givens R,O; M2 uses F,F' (M1); M3 uses R,X (M2); M4 uses V,V' (M3); M5 uses Z (M4) + givens O,R; M6 uses W,W' (M5); M7 uses Z' (M6) + X (M2), R; M8 uses O, U (M7); M9 uses Z', O and line OU (M8); M10/M11 use O, O'' (M9); M12 uses the two free intersections of M10/M11 circles and line OU (M8); M13 uses Z', F,F' (M1) and line WW' (M6); M14 uses Z', U (M7), H (M12) and given line PR; M15 uses G (M13), K (M14). Strictly increasing, no forward references, no phantom points.

findings.claim: Every claimed intersection actually exists (Task 3, part 2)
findings.detail: Verified numerically and by the two-circle criterion |r1−r2| < d < r1+r2. M1: radii 1,1, d=1 → meet; F=(1/2,±√3/2) on both. M3: radii 1, 1/2, d=1 → meet at x=7/8. M5: radii 1,1, d=7/8 → meet at x=7/16. M10/M11: r1=r2=d=|OO''|≈0.576 → meet (equilateral). Circle–line: M7, M13, M14 circles are centered ON the cutting line (WW' is vertical x=7/16 through Z'; PR passes through Z'), so two intersections are guaranteed. M9: line OU is not tangent to circle(Z', 7/16) — perpendicular distance from Z' to OU is 0.3293 < 0.4375 — so the second point O'' exists; also |Z'O''|=7/16 checks. M12: perpendicular bisector of OO'' meets line OU (they are perpendicular, not parallel) at the midpoint of chord OO'', which by the chord-bisection theorem IS the foot of the perpendicular from Z' to OU — the claimed characterization of H is geometrically forced, |UH| = 4/√113 ≈ 0.37629 confirmed, and H is strictly interior (|OH| ≈ 0.288 < |OU| ≈ 0.664).

findings.claim: Strict-Euclid nicety: G lies on the PRODUCTION of chord WW', not on the segment (found while hunting violations)
findings.detail: This is the closest thing to a violation I found, and it is only a strict-mode caveat. G = (7/16, √3 ≈ 1.732) but W = (7/16, √207/16 ≈ 0.899), so G is beyond W on the extension of segment WW'. Under the stated metric this is a non-issue (a straightedge 'line through two points' is the infinite line), and even in Euclid it is free via Postulate 2 (produce the segment), but a maximally strict segment-counting regime would charge one production here. Notably it is the ONLY such spot: O'' and H lie inside segment OU (0.576 and 0.288 < 0.664), K ≈ (0.814, 0) lies inside the given diameter PR, and Z, Z', X lie inside their defining chords. No violation under either stated rule set.

findings.claim: Givens match Ramanujan 1913, apples-to-apples (Task 4)
findings.detail: Ramanujan's verbatim givens are exactly: a circle with centre O and a diameter PR (hence P and R on the circle). The letter Q in 'circle PQR' is NOT an extra given — it is defined mid-construction as the intersection of the perpendicular at T with the circle ('Draw TQ perpendicular to PR'). The spec's givens (circle, centre O, diameter line PR, points P, R) are identical, so the starting inventories match. One fairness note on the COMPARISON rather than the givens: Ramanujan's sentence-steps are macro-operations under this metric (bisection, trisection, erecting a perpendicular, two parallels, placing chords of transferred length, a tangent of specified length — each costs multiple primitive moves), so '15 moves' can only be compared against Ramanujan's construction after compiling his steps to the same primitive-move metric, not against his sentence count. Both constructions target the same value: 355/113 = 3 + 16/113.

findings.claim: The four transfer radii cite segments whose endpoints exist before use, per the metric's wording
findings.detail: Metric (b) requires 'radius equals the distance between two already-constructed points'. M5: O, R are givens (free). M7: X (M2), R (given). M13: F, F' (M1). M14: U (M7), H (M12), both before M14. All four comply. The arithmetic of the final claim is also structurally sound as a legality matter: Z'G is vertical (along WW'), Z'K horizontal (along PR), right angle at Z' is by construction, and |GK|² = FF'² + UH² = 3 + 16/113 = 355/113 (verified 3.1415929204, area rel. error 8.49e-8 as claimed).

findings.claim: Claimed count of 15 moves under the stated metric
findings.detail: Exactly 15 chargeable operations: intersections are free per the metric, choosing 'the upper/right-hand/second' intersection is a free selection among existing free points, moves 10–12 are the standard 3-move perpendicular-bisector gadget, and no hidden operations (no unstated perpendiculars, parallels, or bisections) are smuggled in — everything is either an explicit line, an explicit circle, or a free intersection. 15 is the true count under the modern rigid-compass metric.

findings.claim: Strict-Euclid verdict: the 15-move claim does not survive collapse-compass rules (Task 5(ii))
findings.detail: Under strict Euclid (collapsing compass, I.2/I.3 available as theorems): the construction remains fully LEGAL and produces the same points, but moves 5, 7, 13, 14 each expand to ~7 primitive moves, giving ~39 total (~39–47 with I.2 case handling), plus the free Postulate-2 production of WW' for G. So strictly: constructible, correct, but not '15 moves' — the count is metric-relative, which the spec does disclose ('modern rigid compass: distance transfer = one move').

legality.summary: I tried hard to break this construction and could not find a rules violation under its stated metric. All 15 moves are legal: 6 straightedge lines (2,4,6,8,12,15), 5 collapsing-legal circles (1,3,9,10,11 — note 1 and 3 are 'about R through O/X', hence legal even for a collapsing compass), and 4 genuine rigid-compass transfers (5,7,13,14), each correctly flagged as a transfer in the spec and each irreducible to a collapsing move (no pre-existing point sits at the needed distance from the center). Dependency order is strictly clean; every claimed intersection exists (all circle-circle pairs satisfy |r1−r2| < d < r1+r2; all circle-line cuts are through-center or verified non-tangent, e.g. move 9's second point O'' exists since dist(Z', OU) ≈ 0.329 < 7/16). The move-12 device is legitimate and clever: the perpendicular bisector of chord OO'' of circle(Z') necessarily meets line OU at the foot of the perpendicular from Z', giving |UH| = 4/√113 in 4 moves. Givens match Ramanujan 1913 exactly (circle, centre O, diameter PR; the Q of 'PQR' is constructed, not given). Verdicts: (i) modern rigid-compass rules — VALID, honestly counted at 15 moves; (ii) strict Euclid — VALID for constructibility after I.2 expansion, but the count balloons to roughly 39 (~39–47) primitive moves since each transfer costs ~7 collapsing-legal moves, and one free Postulate-2 production is needed because G lies beyond W on the extension of chord WW' (the only extension in the whole construction — everything else, including K, H, and O'', lands inside already-drawn segments). Caveats, not violations: the 15-move headline is metric-dependent, and any comparison to Ramanujan's step count must first compile his macro-steps (parallels, tangent, chord placements) into the same primitive-move metric.

findings.claim: Final result: |GK| = sqrt(355/113), square on GK approximates circle area with relative error ~8.5e-8
findings.detail: |GK| = 1.772453926158303 (15 digits: 1.77245392615830). |GK| - sqrt(355/113) = 0.0 at 50-digit precision (residual ~5e-51 at the GK^2 level, pure arithmetic noise); float64 replay gives the identical value. |GK| - sqrt(pi) = +7.52527867687938e-8. GK^2 = 3.14159292035398230088 = 355/113 exactly; relative area error (GK^2 - pi)/pi = 8.491367866e-8, matching the claimed ~8.5e-8; relative length error 4.246e-8. Note the construction squares the circle only approximately (Milü 355/113), as the spec itself states.

findings.claim: Move 1: circle(R,1) cuts unit circle in two points F,F' = (1/2, +-sqrt(3)/2)
findings.detail: Circle-circle: d(centers)=1, r1+r2=2, |r1-r2|=0, so 0 < 1 < 2: two distinct points. Recomputed points match claimed coordinates to 0 error. |FF'| = sqrt(3) exactly.

findings.claim: Move 2: line FF' cuts PR at X = (1/2,0)
findings.detail: Line-line denominator 3.464 (non-parallel, unique point). X = (0.5, 0) exactly, midpoint of OR.

findings.claim: Move 3: circle(R, 1/2) cuts unit circle in two points at x = 7/8
findings.detail: |XR| = 1/2 exactly. Circle-circle: d=1, sum=1.5, diff=0.5, so 0.5 < 1 < 1.5: two distinct points, V,V' = (0.875, +-0.48412291827592711).

findings.claim: Move 4: line VV' cuts PR at Z = (7/8, 0)
findings.detail: Non-parallel (denominator 1.936); Z = (0.875, 0) exactly.

findings.claim: Move 5: circle(Z, radius OR=1) cuts unit circle in two points at x = 7/16
findings.detail: Circle-circle: d=0.875, sum=2, diff=0: two distinct points W,W' = (0.4375, +-sqrt(207)/16 = +-0.89921841062113491). Caveat: radius OR is transferred from a remote segment, which is a single move only with a rigid (non-collapsing) compass; the spec acknowledges the transfer.

findings.claim: Move 6: line WW' cuts PR at Z' = (7/16, 0)
findings.detail: Non-parallel (denominator 3.597); Z' = (0.4375, 0) exactly.

findings.claim: Move 7: circle(Z', 1/2) cuts line WW' at upper point U = (7/16, 1/2)
findings.detail: Line passes through the circle's center (dist=0 < r=0.5, discriminant 3.234): two distinct points (7/16, +-1/2). 'Upper' unambiguously selects U = (0.4375, 0.5), which also lies inside chord WW' (|0.5| < 0.899). Transferred radius XR: same rigid-compass caveat as move 5.

findings.claim: Move 8: |OU| = sqrt(113)/16
Move 9: circle(Z', |Z'O|=7/16) cuts line OU 'again' at O'' distinct from O
findings.detail: Discriminant 0.1465 > 0; dist(Z', line OU) = 0.329252 < r = 0.4375: two distinct points. One coincides with O to exactly 0; 'again' unambiguously selects the other, O'' = (0.37942477876106195, 0.43362831858407080).

findings.claim: Moves 10-11: circle(O,|OO''|) and circle(O'',|O''O|) intersect in two points
findings.detail: Equal radii = d(centers) = |OO''| = 0.576192; 0 < d < 2d: two distinct points, symmetric about line OO'' (which is line OU). No selection needed since move 12 uses both.

findings.claim: Move 12: perpendicular bisector of OO'' cuts OU at H = foot of perpendicular from Z' to OU, strictly between O and U, |UH| = 4/sqrt(113)
findings.detail: Line-line non-parallel (denominator 0.663). H = (0.18971238938053097, 0.21681415929203540); independently computed foot of the perpendicular from Z' onto OU agrees to 7e-52. Parameter of H along O->U is t = 0.43363, strictly in (0,1). |UH| = 0.37628834735343892 = 4/sqrt(113) exactly. The geometric reason checks out: O and O'' both lie on circle(Z') and on line OU, so the chord midpoint is the foot of the perpendicular from Z'.

findings.claim: Move 13: circle(Z', sqrt(3)) cuts line WW' at upper point G = (7/16, sqrt(3))
findings.detail: Line through center (dist=0 < r=1.7320508, discriminant 38.81): two distinct points; 'upper' selects G = (0.4375, 1.73205080756887729), matching claim exactly. Caveat: G lies OUTSIDE segment WW' (G.y = 1.732 > W.y = 0.899), so the prose's 'line WW'' must be read as the infinite line, not the chord — the wording 'line' is technically correct but this is the one step where a segment reading would fail. Transferred radius FF': rigid-compass caveat.

findings.claim: Move 14: circle(Z', |UH|) cuts line PR at right-hand point K = (7/16 + 4/sqrt(113), 0)
findings.detail: Line PR passes through center Z' (dist=0 < r=0.376288, discriminant 2.265): two distinct points at x = 7/16 +- 4/sqrt(113) = 0.813788 and 0.061212. Both have positive x and both lie inside segment PR, so 'right-hand' (larger x) is essential and does resolve the choice: K = (0.81378834735343892, 0). Transferred radius UH: rigid-compass caveat.
Right angle at Z' verified: (G-Z').(K-Z') = 0.0 exactly (Z'G vertical, Z'K horizontal). |Z'G|^2 = 3.0 exactly, |Z'K|^2 = 0.14159292035398230 = 16/113 exactly, |GK|^2 = 355/113 to 50 digits.

findings.claim: Ambiguity audit: all stated selectors resolve their choices
findings.detail: Genuinely ambiguous labels: F vs F' (move 1) and V/V', W/W' — the prose never says which is which, but every downstream use (line through the pair, length of the pair) is symmetric, so it is harmless. 'Upper' (moves 7, 13), 'again'/distinct-from-O (move 9), and 'right-hand' (move 14) each cleanly disambiguate a real two-point choice; move 14 is the only one where BOTH candidates lie in the same quadrant of the line, making the selector load-bearing. Move 12 uses both circle intersections to define a line, so no choice arises there.

findings.claim: Dependency audit: no step uses an object not yet constructed
findings.detail: Move 5 uses OR (givens); move 7 uses XR (X from move 2); moves 9 and 12 use line OU (move 8); move 13 uses FF' (moves 1-2); move 14 uses UH (U from move 7, H from move 12). All references point backward. The only accounting caveat: the '15 moves' count treats each remote distance transfer (moves 5, 7, 13, 14) as one move, which presumes a rigid compass; with a classical collapsing compass each transfer costs extra moves (constructibility unaffected by the compass-equivalence theorem, and the spec labels the transfers explicitly).

sim.summary: Independent numerical replay (50-digit mpmath plus float64 cross-check, /tmp/audit-squircle2/verify.py) confirms the construction end to end: every intersection exists with two distinct points exactly where claimed, every parenthetical coordinate and length is reproduced to arithmetic precision without being hardcoded, and the final segment satisfies |GK| = 1.772453926158303 = sqrt(355/113) exactly (difference 0.0 at 50 digits), with |GK| - sqrt(pi) = +7.52527867687938e-8 and relative area error 8.491e-8, matching the claimed ~8.5e-8. H is verified to be the foot of the perpendicular from Z' onto OU with |UH| = 4/sqrt(113), strictly between O and U, and the right angle at Z' makes |GK|^2 = 3 + 16/113 = 355/113 exact. Caveats, none fatal: (1) the 15-move count assumes a rigid compass — moves 5, 7, 13, 14 transfer distances from remote segments, which a collapsing compass cannot do in one move; (2) in move 13, G lies outside the chord WW' (y = sqrt(3) > 0.899), so 'line WW'' must be read as the infinite line — the prose's wording supports this but it is the one step where a segment reading breaks; (3) move 14's 'right-hand' selector is load-bearing since both candidate points lie on the positive x-axis inside PR; (4) the F/F' (and V/V', W/W') labels are never assigned, but all downstream uses are symmetric in the pair, so this is harmless. This is, as expected, an approximate quadrature via the Milü fraction 355/113, not an exact squaring of the circle — the spec claims exactly that and the claim holds.
